```{r}
library(MASS) # for mvrnorm
library(betareg)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggsci)
```

```{r}
set.seed(123)

n <- 1000 # sample size
mu_beta <- c(0, 0, 0) # means of X1, X2, X3

# Covariance matrix for correlated covariates
Sigma <- matrix(c(
    1, 0.5, 0.3,
    0.5, 1, 0.4,
    0.3, 0.4, 1
), nrow = 3)

# Generate correlated covariates
X <- mvrnorm(n, mu = mu_beta, Sigma = Sigma)
X1 <- X[, 1]
X2 <- X[, 2]
X3 <- X[, 3]

# simulate response
beta <- c(0.5, 0.7, -0.3, 0.2) # beta0, beta1, beta2, beta3

# Linear predictor
eta <- beta[1] + beta[2] * X1 + beta[3] * X2 + beta[4] * X3

# Inverse logit to get mean in (0,1)
mu <- plogis(eta)
phi <- exp(1 + 2 * X1) # precision parameter

# phi = 5

# Simulate beta response
Y <- rbeta(n, shape1 = mu * phi, shape2 = (1 - mu) * phi)


# fit <- betareg(Y ~ X1 + X2 + X3 | X1)
fit <- betareg(Y ~ X1 + X2 + X3)
summary(fit)

cor(X) # correlation matrix of covariates

# Combine response and covariates
sim_data <- data.frame(
    Y = Y,
    X1 = X1,
    X2 = X2,
    X3 = X3
)

# Quick look
head(sim_data)
write.csv(sim_data, "simulated_beta_data.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
x_new <- data.frame(X1 = 0, X2 = 1, X3 = 2)

# Predict conditional mean
mu_hat <- predict(fit, newdata = x_new, type = "response")
mu_hat


plot(X1, Y,
    pch = 16, col = rgb(0, 0, 0, 0.4),
    main = "Heteroskedastic Beta response",
    xlab = "X1", ylab = "Y"
)


ord <- order(X1)

plot(X1, Y, pch = 16, col = rgb(0, 0, 0, 0.3))

lines(X1[ord], mu[ord], col = "blue", lwd = 2)

# add ±2 sd bands

sd_y <- sqrt(mu * (1 - mu) / (1 + phi))

lines(X1[ord], (mu + 2 * sd_y)[ord], col = "red", lty = 2)

lines(X1[ord], (mu - 2 * sd_y)[ord], col = "red", lty = 2)


x_vals <- c(-1, 0, 1)

curve_list <- lapply(x_vals, function(x) {
    phi_x <- exp(1 + 2 * x)

    mu_x <- plogis(x) # or your eta mapping

    y <- seq(0.001, 0.999, length = 200)

    d <- dbeta(y, mu_x * phi_x, (1 - mu_x) * phi_x)

    data.frame(y, d, x = x)
})

df <- do.call(rbind, curve_list)

ggplot(df, aes(y, d, color = factor(x))) +
    geom_line() +
    labs(color = "X1", title = "Beta densities at different X1")
```

```{r}
# y_grid <- seq(min(Y), max(Y), length.out = 200)
y_grid <- seq(0.001, 0.999, length.out = 200)
min(Y)
max(Y)
# Get predicted means (mu) and the estimated shape (dispersion)
mu_hat <- predict(fit, type = "response")
# For Gamma GLM, shape = 1 / dispersion
phi_hat <- predict(fit, type = "precision")

# Calculate the mean density across all observations for each point in the grid
marginal_dens <- sapply(y_grid, function(yi) {
    mean(dbeta(yi,
        shape1 = mu_hat * phi_hat,
        shape2 = (1 - mu_hat) * phi_hat
    ))
})
# empirical KDE data
emp <- density(Y)
df_emp <- data.frame(x = emp$x, y = emp$y)

# analytical marginal data
df_ana <- data.frame(x = y_grid, y = marginal_dens)

# combined plot
p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +
    geom_line(data = df_emp, aes(x = x, y = y, color = "Empirical KDE"), linewidth = 1) +
    geom_line(
        data = df_ana, aes(x = x, y = y, color = "Analytical Marginal"),
        linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c(
        "Empirical KDE" = "black",
        "Analytical Marginal" = "blue"
    )) +
    labs(
        title = "Empirical vs. Analytical Marginal Density",
        x = "y",
        y = "Density",
        color = NULL
    ) +
    theme_bw() +
    scale_color_npg() + # Nature Publishing Group style
    theme(
        legend.position = "top",
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        axis.text = element_text(color = "grey30"),
        axis.title = element_text(color = "grey40"),
        plot.title = element_text(color = "grey10"),
        legend.text = element_text(color = "grey10"),
        legend.title = element_text(color = "grey10"),
        legend.margin = margin(b = -5) # pull legend closer
    )
p
```

```{r}
x_grid <- quantile(X1, probs = c(0.1, 0.5, 0.9))

y_seq <- seq(0.001, 0.999, length = 200)

mu_hat  <- fitted(fit)
phi_hat <- predict(fit, type = "precision")

plot(NULL,
     xlim = c(0, 1),
     ylim = c(0, 8),
     xlab = "y",
     ylab = "Density",
     main = "Misspecified model: identical spread across X1")

cols <- c("red", "blue", "darkgreen")

for (j in 1:3) {
  
  idx <- which.min(abs(X1 - x_grid[j]))
  
  dens <- dbeta(y_seq,
                shape1 = mu_hat[idx] * phi_hat[idx],
                shape2 = (1 - mu_hat[idx]) * phi_hat[idx])
  
  lines(y_seq, dens, col = cols[j], lwd = 2)
}

legend("topright",
       legend = paste0("X1 ≈ ", round(x_grid, 2)),
       col = cols,
       lwd = 2)
```
